text.title: spatial-ui-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns repeatedly observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Always-visible HUD panel locked to head; visionOS store review rejects submission.
antipattern.root-cause: Author followed legacy XR docs; LLM defaulted to HUD pattern.
antipattern.fix: Convert to body-locked + recenter affordance; reserve head-locked for safety telemetry only.

antipattern.symptom: Panel at 0.5 m forces user to focus uncomfortably close; eye strain reports.
antipattern.root-cause: Designer treated 1 m as 'arm's length' and shrank it.
antipattern.fix: Enforce ≥1 m minimum in spec validator. Default 1.5-2.0 m.

antipattern.symptom: Multiple stacked panels cover 60% of horizontal FoV; users report visual fatigue.
antipattern.root-cause: No FoV-occupancy budget; panels grew over releases.
antipattern.fix: Compute primary panel FoV occupancy; cap at 40% in validator.

antipattern.symptom: Buttons sized for desktop (24 pt) miss-tap constantly in spatial UI.
antipattern.root-cause: UI ported from web without spatial scale.
antipattern.fix: Enforce 60 pt minimum at 1 m; scale with distance.
